You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime repository.
The codebase is at version 0.6 (commit: 3720474b).

## Issue to Resolve

Obscure error message in the formatting API
Reported to us in private.
```kotlin
    private val readableFormat = DateTimeComponents.Format {
        year()
        char('-')
        monthNumber()
        char('-')
        dayOfMonth()
        char(' ')
        hour()
        char(':')
        minute()
        char(':')
        second()
        alternativeParsing({}) {
            char(' ')
        }
        alternativeParsing(
            { timeZoneId() },
            { offset(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO) }
        ) {
            offsetHours()
            char(':')
            offsetMinutesOfHour()
            optional {
                char(':')
                offsetSecondsOfMinute()
            }
        }
    }

    public fun format(value: Instant): String {
        return readableFormat.format {
            setDateTime(value.toLocalDateTime(zone))
            timeZoneId = zone.id
        }
    }
```

The error message is unreadable:
```
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Field totalHoursAbs is not set
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeComponents.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/LocalDateFormat.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/LocalTimeFormat.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/UtcOffsetFormat.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldSpec.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3â†’ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4â†’ */
  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.DayOfWeek
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 11â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 14â†’import kotlin.reflect.*
 15â†’
 16â†’/**
 17â†’ * A collection of datetime fields used specifically for parsing and formatting.
 18â†’ *
 19â†’ * Its main purpose is to support complex datetime formats that don't correspond to any of the standard
 20â†’ * entities in the library. For example, a format that includes only the month and the day of the month but not the
 21â†’ * year cannot be represented and parsed as a [LocalDate], but it is valid for a [DateTimeComponents].
 22â†’ * See sample 1.
 23â†’ *
 24â†’ * Another purpose is to support parsing and formatting data with out-of-bounds values. For example, parsing
 25â†’ * `23:59:60` as a [LocalTime] is not possible, but it is possible to parse it as a [DateTimeComponents], adjust the value by
 26â†’ * setting [second] to `59`, and then convert it to a [LocalTime] via [toLocalTime].
 27â†’ * See sample 2.
 28â†’ *
 29â†’ * Because this class has limited applications, constructing it directly is not possible.
 30â†’ * For formatting, use the [format] overload that accepts a lambda with a [DateTimeComponents] receiver.
 31â†’ * See sample 3.
 32â†’ *
 33â†’ * Accessing the fields of this class is not thread-safe.
 34â†’ * Make sure to apply proper synchronization if you are using a single instance from multiple threads.
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.parsingComplexInput
 37â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.parsingInvalidInput
 38â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.simpleFormatting
 39â†’ */
 40â†’public class DateTimeComponents internal constructor(internal val contents: DateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()) {
 41â†’    public companion object {
 42â†’        /**
 43â†’         * Creates a [DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values using [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents].
 44â†’         *
 45â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [DateTimeComponents.Formats].
 46â†’         *
 47â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if parsing using this format is ambiguous.
 48â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.customFormat
 49â†’         */
 50â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 51â†’        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> {
 52â†’            val builder = DateTimeComponentsFormat.Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
 53â†’            block(builder)
 54â†’            return DateTimeComponentsFormat(builder.build())
 55â†’        }
 56â†’    }
 57â†’
 58â†’    /**
 59â†’     * A collection of formats for parsing and formatting [DateTimeComponents] values.
 60â†’     *
 61â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [DateTimeComponents.Format] to create a custom
 62â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values.
 63â†’     */
 64â†’    public object Formats {
 65â†’
 66â†’        /**
 67â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format for dates and times with UTC offset.
 68â†’         *
 69â†’         * The format uses the [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO] format to specify the time zone offset, except that
 70â†’         * specifying the minutes of the offset is optional during parsing (so offsets like `+03` are also allowed).
 71â†’         *
 72â†’         * This format differs from [LocalTime.Formats.ISO] in its time part in that
 73â†’         * specifying the seconds is *not* optional.
 74â†’         *
 75â†’         * Examples of instants in the ISO 8601 format:
 76â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00Z`
 77â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.50Z`
 78â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789Z`
 79â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:00`
 80â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:30:20`
 81â†’         * * `2020-01-01T23:59:59.123456789+01`
 82â†’         * * `+12020-01-31T23:59:59Z`
 83â†’         *
 84â†’         * This format uses the local date, local time, and UTC offset fields of [DateTimeComponents].
 85â†’         *
 86â†’         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.4.2.1b), excluding the format without the offset.
 87â†’         *
 88â†’         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [Instant.toString] produces.
 89â†’         *
 90â†’         * Typically, to use this format, you can simply call [Instant.toString] and [Instant.parse].
 91â†’         * However, by accessing this format directly, you can obtain the UTC offset, which is not returned from [Instant.parse],
 92â†’         * or specify the UTC offset to be formatted.
 93â†’         *
 94â†’         * ```
 95â†’         * val components = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse("2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789+03:00")
 96â†’         * val instant = components.toInstantUsingOffset() // 2020-08-30T15:43:00.123456789Z
 97â†’         * val localDateTime = components.toLocalDateTime() // 2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789
 98â†’         * val offset = components.toUtcOffset() // UtcOffset(hours = 3)
 99â†’         * ```
100â†’         *
101â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.Formats.iso
102â†’         */
103â†’        public val ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
104â†’            date(ISO_DATE)
105â†’            alternativeParsing({
106â†’                char('t')
107â†’            }) {
108â†’                char('T')
109â†’            }
110â†’            hour()
111â†’            char(':')
112â†’            minute()
113â†’            char(':')
114â†’            second()
115â†’            optional {
116â†’                char('.')
117â†’                secondFraction(1, 9)
118â†’            }
119â†’            alternativeParsing({
120â†’                offsetHours()
121â†’            }) {
122â†’                offset(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)
123â†’            }
124â†’        }
125â†’
126â†’        /**
127â†’         * RFC 1123 format for dates and times with UTC offset.
128â†’         *
129â†’         * Examples of valid strings:
130â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 GMT`
131â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 -0300`
132â†’         * * `30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 UT`
133â†’         *
134â†’         * North American and military time zone abbreviations are not supported.
135â†’         *
136â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.Formats.rfc1123parsing
137â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.Formats.rfc1123formatting
138â†’         */
139â†’        public val RFC_1123: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
140â†’            alternativeParsing({
141â†’                // the day of week may be missing
142â†’            }) {
143â†’                dayOfWeek(DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
144â†’                chars(", ")
145â†’            }
146â†’            dayOfMonth(Padding.NONE)
147â†’            char(' ')
148â†’            monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
149â†’            char(' ')
150â†’            year()
151â†’            char(' ')
152â†’            hour()
153â†’            char(':')
154â†’            minute()
155â†’            optional {
156â†’                char(':')
157â†’                second()
158â†’            }
159â†’            chars(" ")
160â†’            alternativeParsing({
161â†’                chars("UT")
162â†’            }, {
163â†’                chars("Z")
164â†’            }) {
165â†’                optional("GMT") {
166â†’                    offset(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)
167â†’                }
168â†’            }
169â†’        }
170â†’    }
171â†’
172â†’    /**
173â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
174â†’     * The [localTime] is written to the [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
175â†’     *
176â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
177â†’     *
178â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.time
179â†’     */
180â†’    public fun setTime(localTime: LocalTime) {
181â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localTime)
182â†’    }
183â†’
184â†’    /**
185â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDate] to this [DateTimeComponents].
186â†’     * The [localDate] is written to the [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], and [dayOfWeek] fields.
187â†’     *
188â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
189â†’     *
190â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.date
191â†’     */
192â†’    public fun setDate(localDate: LocalDate) {
193â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDate)
194â†’    }
195â†’
196â†’    /**
197â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDateTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
198â†’     * The [localDateTime] is written to the
199â†’     * [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], [dayOfWeek],
200â†’     * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
201â†’     *
202â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
203â†’     *
204â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.setDateTime
205â†’     */
206â†’    public fun setDateTime(localDateTime: LocalDateTime) {
207â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDateTime.date)
208â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localDateTime.time)
209â†’    }
210â†’
211â†’    /**
212â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [utcOffset] to this [DateTimeComponents].
213â†’     * The [utcOffset] is written to the [offsetHours], [offsetMinutesOfHour], [offsetSecondsOfMinute], and
214â†’     * [offsetIsNegative] fields.
215â†’     *
216â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
217â†’     *
218â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.offset
219â†’     */
220â†’    public fun setOffset(utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
221â†’        contents.offset.populateFrom(utcOffset)
222â†’    }
223â†’
224â†’    /**
225â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [instant] to this [DateTimeComponents].
226â†’     *
227â†’     * This method is almost always equivalent to the following code:
228â†’     * ```
229â†’     * setDateTime(instant.toLocalDateTime(offset))
230â†’     * setOffset(utcOffset)
231â†’     * ```
232â†’     * However, this also works for instants that are too large to be represented as a [LocalDateTime].
233â†’     *
234â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
235â†’     *
236â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.setDateTimeOffsetInstant
237â†’     */
238â†’    public fun setDateTimeOffset(instant: Instant, utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
239â†’        val smallerInstant = Instant.fromEpochSeconds(
240â†’            instant.epochSeconds % SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS, instant.nanosecondsOfSecond
241â†’        )
242â†’        setDateTime(smallerInstant.toLocalDateTime(utcOffset))
243â†’        setOffset(utcOffset)
244â†’        year = year!! + ((instant.epochSeconds / SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS) * 10000).toInt()
245â†’    }
246â†’
247â†’    /**
248â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDateTime] and [utcOffset] to this [DateTimeComponents].
249â†’     *
250â†’     * A shortcut for calling [setDateTime] and [setOffset] separately.
251â†’     *
252â†’     * If [localDateTime] is obtained from an [Instant] using [LocalDateTime.toInstant], it is recommended to use
253â†’     * [setDateTimeOffset] that accepts an [Instant] directly.
254â†’     *
255â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.setDateTimeOffset
256â†’     */
257â†’    public fun setDateTimeOffset(localDateTime: LocalDateTime, utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
258â†’        setDateTime(localDateTime)
259â†’        setOffset(utcOffset)
260â†’    }
261â†’
262â†’    /**
263â†’     * The year component of the date.
264â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.date
265â†’     */
266â†’    public var year: Int? by contents.date::year
267â†’
268â†’    /**
269â†’     * The number-of-month (1..12) component of the date.
270â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
271â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.date
272â†’     */

274â†’

276â†’     * The month ([Month]) component of the date.
277â†’     *
278â†’     * This is a view of [monthNumber].
279â†’     * Setting it will set [monthNumber], and getting it will return a [Month] instance if [monthNumber] is a valid
280â†’     * month.
281â†’     *
282â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during getting if [monthNumber] is outside the `1..12` range.
283â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.date
284â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsSamples.setMonth
285â†’     */
286â†’    public var month: Month?
287â†’        get() = monthNumber?.let { Month(it) }
288â†’        set(value) {

290â†’        }

293â†’     * The day-of-month component of the date.
294â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.

195â†’         * When formatting, the decimal fraction will add trailing zeroes or be rounded as necessary to always output
196â†’         * exactly the number of digits specified in [fixedLength].
197â†’         * Rounding is performed using the round-toward-zero rounding mode.
198â†’         *
199â†’         * When parsing, exactly [fixedLength] digits will be consumed.
200â†’         *
201â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
202â†’         *
203â†’         * See also the [secondFraction] overload that accepts two parameters, the minimum and maximum length of the
204â†’         * fractional part.
205â†’         *
206â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [fixedLength] is not in the range 1..9.
207â†’         *
208â†’         * @see secondFraction that accepts two parameters.
209â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalTimeFormatSamples.fixedLengthSecondFraction
210â†’         */
211â†’        public fun secondFraction(fixedLength: Int) {
212â†’            secondFraction(fixedLength, fixedLength)
213â†’        }
214â†’
215â†’        /**
216â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat] for the time part.
217â†’         *
218â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalTimeFormatSamples.time
219â†’         */
220â†’        public fun time(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>)
221â†’    }
222â†’
223â†’    /**
224â†’     * Functions specific to the datetime format builders containing the local-date and local-time fields.
225â†’     */
226â†’    public sealed interface WithDateTime : WithDate, WithTime {
227â†’        /**
228â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat] for the datetime part.
229â†’         *
230â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateTimeFormatSamples.dateTime
231â†’         */
232â†’        public fun dateTime(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>)
233â†’    }
234â†’
235â†’    /**
236â†’     * Functions specific to the datetime format builders containing the UTC-offset fields.
237â†’     */
238â†’    public sealed interface WithUtcOffset : DateTimeFormatBuilder {
239â†’        /**
240â†’         * The total number of hours in the UTC offset, including the sign.
241â†’         *
242â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
243â†’         *
244â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
245â†’         *
246â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.UtcOffsetFormatSamples.isoOrGmt
247â†’         */
248â†’        public fun offsetHours(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
249â†’
250â†’        /**
251â†’         * The minute-of-hour of the UTC offset.
252â†’         *
253â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
254â†’         *
255â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
256â†’         *
257â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.UtcOffsetFormatSamples.isoOrGmt
258â†’         */
259â†’        public fun offsetMinutesOfHour(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
260â†’
261â†’        /**
262â†’         * The second-of-minute of the UTC offset.
263â†’         *
264â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
265â†’         *
266â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
267â†’         *
268â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.UtcOffsetFormatSamples.isoOrGmt
269â†’         */
270â†’        public fun offsetSecondsOfMinute(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
271â†’
272â†’        /**
273â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat] for the UTC offset part.
274â†’         *
275â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.UtcOffsetFormatSamples.offset
276â†’         */
277â†’        public fun offset(format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>)
278â†’    }
279â†’
280â†’    /**
281â†’     * Builder for formats for values that have all the datetime components:
282â†’     * date, time, UTC offset, and the timezone ID.
283â†’     */
284â†’    public sealed interface WithDateTimeComponents : WithDateTime, WithUtcOffset {
285â†’        /**
286â†’         * The IANA time zone identifier, for example, "Europe/Berlin".
287â†’         *
288â†’         * When formatting, the timezone identifier is supplied as is, without any validation.
289â†’         * On parsing, [TimeZone.availableZoneIds] is used to validate the identifier.
290â†’         *
291â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsFormatSamples.timeZoneId
292â†’         */
293â†’        public fun timeZoneId()
294â†’
295â†’        /**
296â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat].
297â†’         *
298â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeComponentsFormatSamples.dateTimeComponents
299â†’         */
300â†’        public fun dateTimeComponents(format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>)
301â†’    }
302â†’}
303â†’
304â†’/**
305â†’ * The fractional part of the second without the leading dot.
306â†’ *
307â†’ * When formatting, the decimal fraction will round the number to fit in the specified [maxLength] and will add
308â†’ * trailing zeroes to the specified [minLength].
309â†’ *
310â†’ * Additionally, [grouping] is a list, where the i'th (1-based) element specifies how many trailing zeros to add during
311â†’ * formatting when the number would have i digits.
312â†’ *
313â†’ * When parsing, the parser will require that the fraction is at least [minLength] and at most [maxLength]
314â†’ * digits long.
315â†’ *
316â†’ * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
317â†’ *
318â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [minLength] is greater than [maxLength] or if either is not in the range 1..9.
319â†’ */
320â†’internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.secondFractionInternal(
321â†’    minLength: Int,
322â†’    maxLength: Int,
323â†’    grouping: List<Int>
324â†’) {
325â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
326â†’    when (this) {
327â†’        is AbstractWithTimeBuilder -> addFormatStructureForTime(
328â†’            BasicFormatStructure(FractionalSecondDirective(minLength, maxLength, grouping))
329â†’        )
330â†’    }
331â†’}
332â†’
333â†’/**
334â†’ * A format along with other ways to parse the same portion of the value.
335â†’ *
336â†’ * When parsing, first, [primaryFormat] is used; if parsing the whole string fails using that, the formats
337â†’ * from [alternativeFormats] are tried in order.
338â†’ *
339â†’ * When formatting, the [primaryFormat] is used to format the value, and [alternativeFormats] are ignored.
340â†’ *
341â†’ * Example:
342â†’ * ```
343â†’ * alternativeParsing(
344â†’ *   { dayOfMonth(); char('-'); monthNumber() },
345â†’ *   { monthNumber(); char(' '); dayOfMonth() },
346â†’ * ) { monthNumber(); char('/'); dayOfMonth() }
347â†’ * ```
348â†’ *
349â†’ * This will always format a date as `MM/DD`, but will also accept `DD-MM` and `MM DD`.
350â†’ *
351â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeFormatBuilderSamples.alternativeParsing
352â†’ */
353â†’@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
354â†’public fun <T : DateTimeFormatBuilder> T.alternativeParsing(
355â†’    vararg alternativeFormats: T.() -> Unit,
356â†’    primaryFormat: T.() -> Unit
357â†’): Unit = when (this) {
358â†’    is AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *> ->
359â†’        appendAlternativeParsingImpl(
360â†’            *alternativeFormats as Array<out AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *>.() -> Unit>,
361â†’            mainFormat = primaryFormat as (AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *>.() -> Unit)
362â†’        )
363â†’
364â†’    else -> throw IllegalStateException("impossible")
365â†’}
366â†’
367â†’/**
368â†’ * An optional section.
369â†’ *
370â†’ * When formatting, the section is formatted if the value of any field in the block is not equal to the default value.
371â†’ * Only [optional] calls where all the fields have default values are permitted.
372â†’ * See [alternativeParsing] to parse some fields optionally without introducing a particular formatting behavior.
373â†’ *
374â†’ * Example:
375â†’ * ```
376â†’ * offsetHours(); char(':'); offsetMinutesOfHour()
377â†’ * optional { char(':'); offsetSecondsOfMinute() }
378â†’ * ```
379â†’ *
380â†’ * Here, because seconds have the default value of zero, they are formatted only if they are not equal to zero, so the
381â†’ * UTC offset `+18:30:00` gets formatted as `"+18:30"`, but `+18:30:01` becomes `"+18:30:01"`.
382â†’ *
383â†’ * When parsing, either [format] or, if that fails, the literal [ifZero] are parsed. If the [ifZero] string is parsed,
384â†’ * the values in [format] get assigned their default values.
385â†’ *
386â†’ * [ifZero] defines the string that is used if values are the default ones.
387â†’ *
388â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if not all fields used in [format] have a default value.
389â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeFormatBuilderSamples.optional
390â†’ */
391â†’@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
392â†’public fun <T : DateTimeFormatBuilder> T.optional(ifZero: String = "", format: T.() -> Unit): Unit = when (this) {
393â†’    is AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *> -> appendOptionalImpl(
394â†’        onZero = ifZero,
395â†’        format as (AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *>.() -> Unit)
396â†’    )
397â†’
398â†’    else -> throw IllegalStateException("impossible")
399â†’}
400â†’
401â†’/**
402â†’ * A literal character.
403â†’ *
404â†’ * This is a shorthand for `chars(value.toString())`.
405â†’ *
406â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.DateTimeFormatBuilderSamples.char
407â†’ */
408â†’public fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.char(value: Char): Unit = chars(value.toString())
409â†’
410â†’internal interface AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<Target, ActualSelf> :
411â†’    DateTimeFormatBuilder where ActualSelf : AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<Target, ActualSelf> {
412â†’
413â†’    val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<Target>
414â†’    fun createEmpty(): ActualSelf
415â†’
416â†’    fun appendAlternativeParsingImpl(
417â†’        vararg otherFormats: ActualSelf.() -> Unit,
418â†’        mainFormat: ActualSelf.() -> Unit
419â†’    ) {
420â†’        val others = otherFormats.map { block ->
421â†’            createEmpty().also { block(it) }.actualBuilder.build()
422â†’        }
423â†’        val main = createEmpty().also { mainFormat(it) }.actualBuilder.build()
424â†’        actualBuilder.add(AlternativesParsingFormatStructure(main, others))
425â†’    }
426â†’
427â†’    fun appendOptionalImpl(
428â†’        onZero: String,
429â†’        format: ActualSelf.() -> Unit
430â†’    ) {
431â†’        actualBuilder.add(OptionalFormatStructure(onZero, createEmpty().also { format(it) }.actualBuilder.build()))
432â†’    }
433â†’
434â†’    override fun chars(value: String) = actualBuilder.add(ConstantFormatStructure(value))
435â†’
436â†’    fun build(): CachedFormatStructure<Target> = CachedFormatStructure(actualBuilder.build().formats)
437â†’}
438â†’
439â†’internal fun <T> FormatStructure<T>.builderString(constants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>>): String =
440â†’    when (this) {
441â†’        is BasicFormatStructure -> directive.builderRepresentation
442â†’        is ConstantFormatStructure -> if (string.length == 1) {
443â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder::char.name}(${string[0].toKotlinCode()})"
444â†’        } else {
445â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder::chars.name}(${string.toKotlinCode()})"
446â†’        }
447â†’
448â†’        is SignedFormatStructure -> {
449â†’            if (format is BasicFormatStructure && format.directive is UtcOffsetWholeHoursDirective) {
450â†’                format.directive.builderRepresentation
451â†’            } else {
452â†’                buildString {
453â†’                    if (withPlusSign) appendLine("withSharedSign(outputPlus = true) {")
454â†’                    else appendLine("withSharedSign {")
455â†’                    appendLine(format.builderString(constants).prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
456â†’                    append("}")
457â†’                }
458â†’            }
459â†’        }
460â†’
461â†’        is OptionalFormatStructure -> buildString {
462â†’            if (onZero == "") {
463â†’                appendLine("${DateTimeFormatBuilder::optional.name} {")
464â†’            } else {
465â†’                appendLine("${DateTimeFormatBuilder::optional.name}(${onZero.toKotlinCode()}) {")
466â†’            }
467â†’            val subformat = format.builderString(constants)
468â†’            if (subformat.isNotEmpty()) {
469â†’                appendLine(subformat.prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
470â†’            }
471â†’            append("}")
472â†’        }
473â†’
474â†’        is AlternativesParsingFormatStructure -> buildString {
475â†’            append("${DateTimeFormatBuilder::alternativeParsing.name}(")
476â†’            for (alternative in formats) {
477â†’                appendLine("{")
478â†’                val subformat = alternative.builderString(constants)
479â†’                if (subformat.isNotEmpty()) {
480â†’                    appendLine(subformat.prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
481â†’                }
482â†’                append("}, ")
483â†’            }
484â†’            if (this[length - 2] == ',') {
485â†’                repeat(2) {
486â†’                    deleteAt(length - 1)
487â†’                }
488â†’            }
489â†’            appendLine(") {")
490â†’            appendLine(mainFormat.builderString(constants).prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
491â†’            append("}")
492â†’        }
493â†’
494â†’        is ConcatenatedFormatStructure -> buildString {
495â†’            if (formats.isNotEmpty()) {
496â†’                var index = 0
497â†’                loop@ while (index < formats.size) {
498â†’                    searchConstant@ for (constant in constants) {
499â†’                        val constantDirectives = constant.second.formats
500â†’                        if (formats.size - index >= constantDirectives.size) {
501â†’                            for (i in constantDirectives.indices) {
502â†’                                if (formats[index + i] != constantDirectives[i]) {
503â†’                                    continue@searchConstant
504â†’                                }
505â†’                            }
506â†’                            append(constant.first)
507â†’                            index += constantDirectives.size
508â†’                            if (index < formats.size) {
509â†’                                appendLine()
510â†’                            }
511â†’                            continue@loop
512â†’                        }
513â†’                    }
514â†’                    if (index == formats.size - 1) {
515â†’                        append(formats.last().builderString(constants))
516â†’                    } else {
517â†’                        appendLine(formats[index].builderString(constants))
518â†’                    }
519â†’                    ++index
520â†’                }
521â†’            }
522â†’        }
523â†’    }
524â†’
525â†’private const val CODE_INDENT = "    "
526â†’

1â†’/*

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.AssignableField
  9â†’import kotlin.reflect.KMutableProperty1
 10â†’
 11â†’/**
 12â†’ * A lens-like interface for accessing fields of objects.
 13â†’ */
 14â†’internal interface Accessor<in Object, Field>: AssignableField<Object, Field> {
 15â†’    /**
 16â†’     * The value of the field in the given object, or `null` if the field is not set.
 17â†’     */
 18â†’    fun getter(container: Object): Field?
 19â†’
 20â†’    /**
 21â†’     * Function that returns the value of the field in the given object,
 22â†’     * or throws [IllegalStateException] if the field is not set.
 23â†’     *
 24â†’     * This function is used to access fields during formatting.
 25â†’     */
 26â†’    fun getterNotNull(container: Object): Field =
 27â†’        getter(container) ?: throw IllegalStateException("Field $name is not set")
 28â†’}
 29â†’
 30â†’/**
 31â†’ * An implementation of [Accessor] for a mutable property of an object.
 32â†’ */
 33â†’internal class PropertyAccessor<Object, Field>(private val property: KMutableProperty1<Object, Field?>): Accessor<Object, Field> {
 34â†’    override val name: String get() = property.name
 35â†’
 36â†’    override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Object, newValue: Field): Field? {
 37â†’        val oldValue = property.get(container)
 38â†’        return when {
 39â†’            oldValue === null -> {
 40â†’                property.set(container, newValue)
 41â†’                null
 42â†’            }
 43â†’            oldValue == newValue -> null
 44â†’            else -> oldValue
 45â†’        }
 46â†’    }
 47â†’
 48â†’    override fun getter(container: Object): Field? = property.get(container)
 49â†’}
 50â†’
 51â†’/**
 52â†’ * A description of the field's numeric sign.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * Several fields can share the same sign. For example, the hour and the minute of the UTC offset have the same sign,
 55â†’ * and setting the sign of the hour also sets the sign of the minute.
 56â†’ *
 57â†’ * Implementations of this interface are *not* required to redefine [equals] and [hashCode].
 58â†’ * Instead, signs should be defined as singletons and compared by reference.
 59â†’ */
 60â†’internal interface FieldSign<in Target> {
 61â†’    /**
 62â†’     * The field that is `true` if the value of the field is known to be negative, and `false` otherwise.
 63â†’     * Can be both read from and written to.
 64â†’     */
 65â†’    val isNegative: Accessor<Target, Boolean>
 66â†’
 67â†’    /**
 68â†’     * A check for whether the current value of the field is zero.
 69â†’     */
 70â†’    fun isZero(obj: Target): Boolean
 71â†’}
 72â†’
 73â†’/**
 74â†’ * A specification of a field.
 75â†’ *
 76â†’ * Fields represent parts of objects, regardless of how they are stored or formatted.
 77â†’ * For example, a field "day of week" can be represented with both strings of various kinds ("Monday", "Mon") and
 78â†’ * numbers ("1" for Monday, "2" for Tuesday, etc.), but the field itself is the same.
 79â†’ *
 80â†’ * Fields can typically contain `null` values, which means that the field is not set.
 81â†’ */
 82â†’internal interface FieldSpec<in Target, Type> {
 83â†’    /**
 84â†’     * The function with which the field can be accessed.
 85â†’     */
 86â†’    val accessor: Accessor<Target, Type>
 87â†’
 88â†’    /**
 89â†’     * The default value of the field, or `null` if the field has none.
 90â†’     */
 91â†’    val defaultValue: Type?
 92â†’
 93â†’    /**
 94â†’     * The name of the field.
 95â†’     */
 96â†’    val name: String
 97â†’
 98â†’    /**
 99â†’     * The sign corresponding to the field value, or `null` if the field has none.
100â†’     */
101â†’    val sign: FieldSign<Target>?
102â†’}
103â†’
104â†’/**
105â†’ * Inherit from this class to obtain a sensible [toString] implementation for debugging.
106â†’ */
107â†’internal abstract class AbstractFieldSpec<in Target, Type>: FieldSpec<Target, Type> {
108â†’    override fun toString(): String = "The field $name (default value is $defaultValue)"
109â†’}
110â†’
111â†’/**
112â†’ * A specification of a field that can contain values of any kind.
113â†’ * Used for fields additional information about which is not that important for parsing/formatting.
114â†’ */
115â†’internal class GenericFieldSpec<in Target, Type>(
116â†’    override val accessor: Accessor<Target, Type>,
117â†’    override val name: String = accessor.name,
118â†’    override val defaultValue: Type? = null,
119â†’    override val sign: FieldSign<Target>? = null,
120â†’) : AbstractFieldSpec<Target, Type>()
121â†’
122â†’/**
123â†’ * A specification of a field that can only contain non-negative numeric values.
124â†’ */
125â†’internal class UnsignedFieldSpec<in Target>(
126â†’    override val accessor: Accessor<Target, Int>,
127â†’    /**
128â†’     * The minimum value of the field.
129â†’     */
130â†’    val minValue: Int,
131â†’    /**
132â†’     * The maximum value of the field.
133â†’     */
134â†’    val maxValue: Int,
135â†’    override val name: String = accessor.name,
136â†’    override val defaultValue: Int? = null,
137â†’    override val sign: FieldSign<Target>? = null,
138â†’) : AbstractFieldSpec<Target, Int>() {
139â†’    /**
140â†’     * The maximum length of the field when represented as a decimal number.
141â†’     */
142â†’    val maxDigits: Int = when {
143â†’        maxValue < 10 -> 1
144â†’        maxValue < 100 -> 2
145â†’        maxValue < 1000 -> 3
146â†’        else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Max value $maxValue is too large")
147â†’    }
148â†’}
149â†’

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.formatter.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * A directive that specifies a way to parse and format the [field].
 14â†’ */
 15â†’internal interface FieldFormatDirective<in Target> {
 16â†’    /**
 17â†’     * The field parsed and formatted by this directive.
 18â†’     */
 19â†’    val field: FieldSpec<Target, *>
 20â†’
 21â†’    /**
 22â†’     * The formatter operation that formats the field.
 23â†’     */
 24â†’    fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target>
 25â†’
 26â†’    /**
 27â†’     * The parser structure that parses the field.
 28â†’     */
 29â†’    fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target>
 30â†’
 31â†’    /**
 32â†’     * The string with the code that, when evaluated in the builder context, appends the directive.
 33â†’     */
 34â†’    val builderRepresentation: String
 35â†’}
 36â†’
 37â†’/**
 38â†’ * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 39â†’ * The field is formatted with the field padded to [minDigits] with zeroes,
 40â†’ * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.
 41â†’ */
 42â†’internal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43â†’    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44â†’    private val minDigits: Int,
 45â†’    private val spacePadding: Int?,
 46â†’) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 47â†’
 48â†’    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49â†’
 50â†’    init {
 51â†’        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52â†’            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53â†’        }
 54â†’        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55â†’            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56â†’        }
 57â†’        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58â†’            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59â†’                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60â†’            }
 61â†’        }
 62â†’    }
 63â†’
 64â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65â†’        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66â†’            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67â†’            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68â†’        )
 69â†’        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70â†’    }
 71â†’
 72â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73â†’        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74â†’}
 75â†’
 76â†’/**
 77â†’ * A directive for a string-based format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 78â†’ */
 79â†’internal abstract class NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 80â†’    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 81â†’    private val values: List<String>,
 82â†’    private val name: String,
 83â†’) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 84â†’
 85â†’    init {
 86â†’        require(values.size == field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1) {
 87â†’            "The number of values (${values.size}) in $values does not match the range of the field (${field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1})"
 88â†’        }
 89â†’    }
 90â†’
 91â†’    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
 92â†’        values.getOrNull(it-field.minValue)
 93â†’            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
 94â†’    }
 95â†’
 96â†’    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97â†’        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98â†’            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {
 99â†’                values[it - field.minValue]
100â†’            }
101â†’
102â†’        override val name: String get() = this@NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective.name
103â†’    }
104â†’
105â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
106â†’        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
107â†’
108â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
109â†’        ParserStructure(
110â†’            listOf(
111â†’                StringSetParserOperation(values, AssignableString(), "one of $values for $name")
112â†’            ), emptyList()
113â†’        )
114â†’}
115â†’
116â†’/**
117â†’ * A directive for a string-based format of an enum field.
118â†’ */
119â†’internal abstract class NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target, Type>(
120â†’    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Type>,
121â†’    private val mapping: Map<Type, String>,
122â†’    private val name: String,
123â†’) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
124â†’
125â†’    private val reverseMapping = mapping.entries.associate { it.value to it.key }
126â†’
127â†’    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
128â†’        mapping[field.accessor.getterNotNull(target)]
129â†’            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
130â†’    }
131â†’
132â†’    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
133â†’        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
134â†’            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, reverseMapping[newValue]!!)?.let { mapping[it] }
135â†’
136â†’        override val name: String get() = this@NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective.name
137â†’    }
138â†’
139â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
140â†’        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
141â†’
142â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
143â†’        ParserStructure(
144â†’            listOf(
145â†’                StringSetParserOperation(mapping.values, AssignableString(), "one of ${mapping.values} for $name")
146â†’            ), emptyList()
147â†’        )
148â†’}
149â†’
150â†’internal abstract class StringFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
151â†’    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, String>,
152â†’    private val acceptedStrings: Set<String>,
153â†’) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
154â†’
155â†’    init {
156â†’        require(acceptedStrings.isNotEmpty()) {
157â†’            "The set of accepted strings is empty"
158â†’        }
159â†’    }
160â†’
161â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
162â†’        StringFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull)
163â†’
164â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
165â†’        ParserStructure(
166â†’            listOf(StringSetParserOperation(acceptedStrings, field.accessor, field.name)),
167â†’            emptyList()
168â†’        )
169â†’}
170â†’
171â†’internal abstract class SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
172â†’    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
173â†’    private val minDigits: Int?,
174â†’    private val maxDigits: Int?,
175â†’    private val spacePadding: Int?,
176â†’    private val outputPlusOnExceededWidth: Int?,
177â†’) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
178â†’
179â†’    init {
180â†’        require(minDigits == null || minDigits >= 0) { "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative" }
181â†’        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {
182â†’            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
183â†’        }
184â†’    }
185â†’
186â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187â†’        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(
188â†’            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
189â†’            zeroPadding = minDigits ?: 0,
190â†’            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191â†’        )
192â†’        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
193â†’    }
194â†’
195â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
196â†’        SignedIntParser(
197â†’            minDigits = minDigits,
198â†’            maxDigits = maxDigits,
199â†’            spacePadding = spacePadding,
200â†’            field.accessor,
201â†’            field.name,
202â†’            plusOnExceedsWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
203â†’        )
204â†’}
205â†’
206â†’internal abstract class DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
207â†’    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, DecimalFraction>,
208â†’    private val minDigits: Int,
209â†’    private val maxDigits: Int,
210â†’    private val zerosToAdd: List<Int>,
211â†’) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213â†’        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214â†’
215â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216â†’        listOf(
217â†’            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218â†’                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219â†’            )
220â†’        ),
221â†’        emptyList()
222â†’    )
223â†’}
224â†’
225â†’internal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226â†’    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227â†’    private val digits: Int,
228â†’    private val base: Int,
229â†’) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230â†’
231â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
232â†’        ReducedIntFormatterStructure(
233â†’            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
234â†’            digits = digits,
235â†’            base = base,
236â†’        )
237â†’
238â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239â†’        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240â†’}

 15â†’/**
 16â†’ * A description of how month names are formatted.
 17â†’ *
 18â†’ * Instances of this class are typically used as arguments to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.monthName].
 19â†’ *
 20â†’ * Predefined instances are available as [ENGLISH_FULL] and [ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED].
 21â†’ * You can also create custom instances using the constructor.
 22â†’ *
 23â†’ * An [IllegalArgumentException] will be thrown if some month name is empty or there are duplicate names.
 24â†’ *
 25â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.usage
 26â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.constructionFromList
 27â†’ */
 28â†’public class MonthNames(
 29â†’    /**
 30â†’     * A list of month names in order from January to December.
 31â†’     *
 32â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.names
 33â†’     */
 34â†’    public val names: List<String>
 35â†’) {
 36â†’    init {
 37â†’        require(names.size == 12) { "Month names must contain exactly 12 elements" }
 38â†’        names.indices.forEach { ix ->
 39â†’            require(names[ix].isNotEmpty()) { "A month name can not be empty" }
 40â†’            for (ix2 in 0 until ix) {
 41â†’                require(names[ix] != names[ix2]) {
 42â†’                    "Month names must be unique, but '${names[ix]}' was repeated"
 43â†’                }
 44â†’            }
 45â†’        }
 46â†’    }
 47â†’
 48â†’    /**
 49â†’     * Create a [MonthNames] using the month names in order from January to December.
 50â†’     *
 51â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.constructionFromStrings
 52â†’     */
 53â†’    public constructor(
 54â†’        january: String, february: String, march: String, april: String, may: String, june: String,
 55â†’        july: String, august: String, september: String, october: String, november: String, december: String
 56â†’    ) :
 57â†’        this(listOf(january, february, march, april, may, june, july, august, september, october, november, december))
 58â†’
 59â†’    public companion object {
 60â†’        /**
 61â†’         * English month names from 'January' to 'December'.
 62â†’         *
 63â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.englishFull
 64â†’         */
 65â†’        public val ENGLISH_FULL: MonthNames = MonthNames(
 66â†’            listOf(
 67â†’                "January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June",
 68â†’                "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December"
 69â†’            )
 70â†’        )
 71â†’
 72â†’        /**
 73â†’         * Shortened English month names from 'Jan' to 'Dec'.
 74â†’         *
 75â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.MonthNamesSamples.englishAbbreviated
 76â†’         */
 77â†’        public val ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED: MonthNames = MonthNames(
 78â†’            listOf(
 79â†’                "Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
 80â†’                "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"
 81â†’            )
 82â†’        )
 83â†’    }
 84â†’
 85â†’    /** @suppress */
 86â†’    override fun toString(): String =
 87â†’        names.joinToString(", ", "MonthNames(", ")", transform = String::toString)
 88â†’
 89â†’    /** @suppress */
 90â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthNames && names == other.names
 91â†’
 92â†’    /** @suppress */
 93â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.hashCode()
 94â†’}
 95â†’
 96â†’private fun MonthNames.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this.names) {
 97â†’    MonthNames.ENGLISH_FULL.names -> "MonthNames.${MonthNames.Companion::ENGLISH_FULL.name}"
 98â†’    MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED.names -> "MonthNames.${MonthNames.Companion::ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED.name}"
 99â†’    else -> names.joinToString(", ", "MonthNames(", ")", transform = String::toKotlinCode)
100â†’}
101â†’
102â†’/**
103â†’ * A description of how the names of weekdays are formatted.
104â†’ *
105â†’ * Instances of this class are typically used as arguments to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.dayOfWeek].
106â†’ *
107â†’ * Predefined instances are available as [ENGLISH_FULL] and [ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED].
108â†’ * You can also create custom instances using the constructor.
109â†’ *
110â†’ * An [IllegalArgumentException] will be thrown if some day-of-week name is empty or there are duplicate names.
111â†’ *
112â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.DayOfWeekNamesSamples.usage
113â†’ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.DayOfWeekNamesSamples.constructionFromList
114â†’ */
115â†’public class DayOfWeekNames(
116â†’    /**
117â†’     * A list of the names of weekdays in order from Monday to Sunday.
118â†’     *
119â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.DayOfWeekNamesSamples.names
120â†’     */
121â†’    public val names: List<String>
122â†’) {
123â†’    init {
124â†’        require(names.size == 7) { "Day of week names must contain exactly 7 elements" }
125â†’        names.indices.forEach { ix ->
126â†’            require(names[ix].isNotEmpty()) { "A day-of-week name can not be empty" }
127â†’            for (ix2 in 0 until ix) {
128â†’                require(names[ix] != names[ix2]) {
129â†’                    "Day-of-week names must be unique, but '${names[ix]}' was repeated"
130â†’                }
131â†’            }
132â†’        }
133â†’    }
134â†’
135â†’    /**
136â†’     * A constructor that takes the names of weekdays in order from Monday to Sunday.
137â†’     *
138â†’     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.DayOfWeekNamesSamples.constructionFromStrings
139â†’     */
140â†’    public constructor(
141â†’        monday: String,
142â†’        tuesday: String,
143â†’        wednesday: String,
144â†’        thursday: String,
145â†’        friday: String,
146â†’        saturday: String,
147â†’        sunday: String
148â†’    ) :
149â†’        this(listOf(monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday, sunday))
150â†’
151â†’    public companion object {
152â†’        /**
153â†’         * English names of weekdays from 'Monday' to 'Sunday'.
154â†’         *
155â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.DayOfWeekNamesSamples.englishFull
156â†’         */
157â†’        public val ENGLISH_FULL: DayOfWeekNames = DayOfWeekNames(
158â†’            listOf(
159â†’                "Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"
160â†’            )
161â†’        )
162â†’
163â†’        /**
164â†’         * Shortened English names of weekdays from 'Mon' to 'Sun'.
165â†’         *
166â†’         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.format.LocalDateFormatSamples.DayOfWeekNamesSamples.englishAbbreviated
167â†’         */
168â†’        public val ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED: DayOfWeekNames = DayOfWeekNames(
169â†’            listOf(
170â†’                "Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun"
171â†’            )
172â†’        )
173â†’    }
174â†’
175â†’    /** @suppress */
176â†’    override fun toString(): String =
177â†’        names.joinToString(", ", "DayOfWeekNames(", ")", transform = String::toString)
178â†’
179â†’    /** @suppress */
180â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfWeekNames && names == other.names
181â†’
182â†’    /** @suppress */
183â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.hashCode()
184â†’}
185â†’
186â†’private fun DayOfWeekNames.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this.names) {
187â†’    DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_FULL.names -> "DayOfWeekNames.${DayOfWeekNames.Companion::ENGLISH_FULL.name}"
188â†’    DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED.names -> "DayOfWeekNames.${DayOfWeekNames.Companion::ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED.name}"
189â†’    else -> names.joinToString(", ", "DayOfWeekNames(", ")", transform = String::toKotlinCode)
190â†’}

192â†’internal fun <T> requireParsedField(field: T?, name: String): T {
193â†’    if (field == null) {
194â†’        throw DateTimeFormatException("Can not create a $name from the given input: the field $name is missing")
195â†’    }
196â†’    return field
197â†’}
198â†’
199â†’internal interface DateFieldContainer {
200â†’    var year: Int?
201â†’    var monthNumber: Int?
202â†’    var dayOfMonth: Int?
203â†’    var isoDayOfWeek: Int?
204â†’    var dayOfYear: Int?
205â†’}
206â†’
207â†’private object DateFields {
208â†’    val year = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::year))
209â†’    val month = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::monthNumber), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
210â†’    val dayOfMonth = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfMonth), minValue = 1, maxValue = 31)
211â†’    val isoDayOfWeek = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::isoDayOfWeek), minValue = 1, maxValue = 7)
212â†’    val dayOfYear = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfYear), minValue = 1, maxValue = 366)
213â†’}
214â†’
215â†’/**
216â†’ * A [kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate], but potentially incomplete and inconsistent.
217â†’ */
218â†’internal class IncompleteLocalDate(
219â†’    override var year: Int? = null,
220â†’    override var monthNumber: Int? = null,
221â†’    override var dayOfMonth: Int? = null,
222â†’    override var isoDayOfWeek: Int? = null,
223â†’    override var dayOfYear: Int? = null,
224â†’) : DateFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalDate> {
225â†’    fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate {
226â†’        val year = requireParsedField(year, "year")
227â†’        val date = when (val dayOfYear = dayOfYear) {
228â†’            null -> LocalDate(
229â†’                year,
230â†’                requireParsedField(monthNumber, "monthNumber"),
231â†’                requireParsedField(dayOfMonth, "dayOfMonth")
232â†’            )
233â†’            else -> LocalDate(year, 1, 1).plus(dayOfYear - 1, DateTimeUnit.DAY).also {
234â†’                if (it.year != year) {
235â†’                    throw DateTimeFormatException(
236â†’                        "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
237â†’                            "the day of year is $dayOfYear, which is not a valid day of year for the year $year"
238â†’                    )
239â†’                }
240â†’                if (monthNumber != null && it.monthNumber != monthNumber) {
241â†’                    throw DateTimeFormatException(
242â†’                        "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
243â†’                            "the day of year is $dayOfYear, which is ${it.month}, " +
244â†’                                "but $monthNumber was specified as the month number"
245â†’                    )
246â†’                }
247â†’                if (dayOfMonth != null && it.dayOfMonth != dayOfMonth) {
248â†’                    throw DateTimeFormatException(
249â†’                        "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
250â†’                            "the day of year is $dayOfYear, which is the day ${it.dayOfMonth} of ${it.month}, " +
251â†’                                "but $dayOfMonth was specified as the day of month"
252â†’                    )
253â†’                }
254â†’            }
255â†’        }
256â†’        isoDayOfWeek?.let {
257â†’            if (it != date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber) {
258â†’                throw DateTimeFormatException(
259â†’                    "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
260â†’                        "the day of week is ${DayOfWeek(it)} but the date is $date, which is a ${date.dayOfWeek}"
261â†’                )
262â†’            }
263â†’        }
264â†’        return date
265â†’    }
266â†’
267â†’    fun populateFrom(date: LocalDate) {
268â†’        year = date.year
269â†’        monthNumber = date.monthNumber
270â†’        dayOfMonth = date.dayOfMonth
271â†’        isoDayOfWeek = date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber
272â†’        dayOfYear = date.dayOfYear
273â†’    }
274â†’
275â†’    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalDate =
276â†’        IncompleteLocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, isoDayOfWeek, dayOfYear)
277â†’
278â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
279â†’        other is IncompleteLocalDate && year == other.year && monthNumber == other.monthNumber &&
280â†’            dayOfMonth == other.dayOfMonth && isoDayOfWeek == other.isoDayOfWeek && dayOfYear == other.dayOfYear
281â†’
282â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = year.hashCode() * 923521 +
283â†’            monthNumber.hashCode() * 29791 +
284â†’            dayOfMonth.hashCode() * 961 +
285â†’            isoDayOfWeek.hashCode() * 31 +
286â†’            dayOfYear.hashCode()
287â†’
288â†’    override fun toString(): String =
289â†’        "${year ?: "??"}-${monthNumber ?: "??"}-${dayOfMonth ?: "??"} (day of week is ${isoDayOfWeek ?: "??"})"
290â†’}
291â†’
292â†’private class YearDirective(private val padding: Padding, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
293â†’    SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
294â†’        DateFields.year,
295â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(4),
296â†’        maxDigits = null,
297â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(4),
298â†’        outputPlusOnExceededWidth = 4,
299â†’    ) {
300â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
301â†’        get() = when (padding) {
302â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}()"
303â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
304â†’        }.let {
305â†’            if (isYearOfEra) {
306â†’                it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
307â†’            } else it
308â†’        }
309â†’
310â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
311â†’        other is YearDirective && padding == other.padding && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
312â†’
313â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
314â†’}
315â†’
316â†’private class ReducedYearDirective(val base: Int, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
317â†’    ReducedIntFieldDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
318â†’        DateFields.year,
319â†’        digits = 2,
320â†’        base = base,
321â†’    ) {
322â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
323â†’        get() =
324â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::yearTwoDigits.name}($base)".let {
325â†’                if (isYearOfEra) {
326â†’                    it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
327â†’                } else it
328â†’            }
329â†’
330â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
331â†’        other is ReducedYearDirective && base == other.base && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
332â†’
333â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = base.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
334â†’}
335â†’
336â†’private const val YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT =
337â†’    " /** TODO: the original format had an `y` directive, so the behavior is different on years earlier than 1 AD. See the [kotlinx.datetime.format.byUnicodePattern] documentation for details. */"
338â†’
339â†’/**
340â†’ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
341â†’ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
342â†’ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
343â†’ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
344â†’ */
345â†’internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEra(padding: Padding) {
346â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
347â†’    when (this) {
348â†’        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
349â†’            BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding, isYearOfEra = true))
350â†’        )
351â†’    }
352â†’}
353â†’
354â†’/**
355â†’ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
356â†’ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
357â†’ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
358â†’ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
359â†’ */
360â†’internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEraTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) {
361â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
362â†’    when (this) {
363â†’        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
364â†’            BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear, isYearOfEra = true))
365â†’        )
366â†’    }
367â†’}
368â†’
369â†’private class MonthDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
370â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
371â†’        DateFields.month,
372â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
373â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
374â†’    ) {
375â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
376â†’        get() = when (padding) {
377â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}()"
378â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
379â†’        }
380â†’
381â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthDirective && padding == other.padding
382â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
383â†’}
384â†’
385â†’private class MonthNameDirective(private val names: MonthNames) :
386â†’    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.month, names.names, "monthName") {
387â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
388â†’        get() =
389â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthName.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
390â†’
391â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthNameDirective && names.names == other.names.names
392â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
393â†’}
394â†’
395â†’private class DayDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
396â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
397â†’        DateFields.dayOfMonth,
398â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
399â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
400â†’    ) {
401â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
402â†’        get() = when (padding) {
403â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}()"
404â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
405â†’        }
406â†’
407â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayDirective && padding == other.padding
408â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
409â†’}
410â†’
411â†’private class DayOfYearDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
412â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
413â†’        DateFields.dayOfYear,
414â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(3),
415â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(3),
416â†’    ) {
417â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
418â†’        get() = when (padding) {
419â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfYear.name}()"
420â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfYear.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
421â†’        }
422â†’
423â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfYearDirective && padding == other.padding
424â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
425â†’}
426â†’
427â†’private class DayOfWeekDirective(private val names: DayOfWeekNames) :
428â†’    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.isoDayOfWeek, names.names, "dayOfWeekName") {
429â†’
430â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
431â†’        get() =
432â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfWeek.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
433â†’
434â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfWeekDirective && names.names == other.names.names
435â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
436â†’}
437â†’
438â†’internal class LocalDateFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
439â†’    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalDate, IncompleteLocalDate>() {
440â†’    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalDate): IncompleteLocalDate =
441â†’        IncompleteLocalDate().apply { populateFrom(value) }
442â†’
443â†’    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalDate): LocalDate = intermediate.toLocalDate()
444â†’
445â†’    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyIncompleteLocalDate
446â†’
447â†’    companion object {
448â†’        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> {
449â†’            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
450â†’            builder.block()
451â†’            return LocalDateFormat(builder.build())
452â†’        }
453â†’    }
454â†’
455â†’    internal class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
456â†’        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithDateBuilder {
457â†’
458â†’        override fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) =
459â†’            actualBuilder.add(structure)
460â†’
461â†’        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
462â†’    }
463â†’}
464â†’
465â†’internal interface AbstractWithDateBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate {
466â†’    fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>)
467â†’
468â†’    override fun year(padding: Padding) =
469â†’        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding)))
470â†’
471â†’    override fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) =
472â†’        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear)))
473â†’
474â†’    override fun monthNumber(padding: Padding) =
475â†’        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthDirective(padding)))
476â†’
477â†’    override fun monthName(names: MonthNames) =
478â†’        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthNameDirective(names)))
479â†’
480â†’    override fun dayOfMonth(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayDirective(padding)))
481â†’    override fun dayOfWeek(names: DayOfWeekNames) =
482â†’        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayOfWeekDirective(names)))
483â†’
484â†’    override fun dayOfYear(padding: Padding) =
485â†’        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayOfYearDirective(padding)))
486â†’
487â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
488â†’    override fun date(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>) = when (format) {
489â†’        is LocalDateFormat -> addFormatStructureForDate(format.actualFormat)
490â†’    }
491â†’}
492â†’
493â†’// these are constants so that the formats are not recreated every time they are used
494â†’internal val ISO_DATE by lazy {
495â†’    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); char('-'); monthNumber(); char('-'); dayOfMonth() }
496â†’}
497â†’internal val ISO_DATE_BASIC by lazy {
498â†’    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); monthNumber(); dayOfMonth() }
499â†’}
500â†’
501â†’private val emptyIncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate()
502â†’

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.LocalTime
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeFormatException
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
 11â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13â†’
 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * The AM/PM marker that indicates whether the hour in range `1..12` is before or after noon.
 16â†’ */
 17â†’public enum class AmPmMarker {
 18â†’    /** The time is before noon. */
 19â†’    AM,
 20â†’
 21â†’    /** The time is after noon. */
 22â†’    PM,
 23â†’}
 24â†’
 25â†’internal interface TimeFieldContainer {
 26â†’    var minute: Int?
 27â†’    var second: Int?
 28â†’    var nanosecond: Int?
 29â†’    var hour: Int?
 30â†’    var hourOfAmPm: Int?
 31â†’    var amPm: AmPmMarker?
 32â†’
 33â†’    var fractionOfSecond: DecimalFraction?
 34â†’        get() = nanosecond?.let { DecimalFraction(it, 9) }
 35â†’        set(value) {
 36â†’            nanosecond = value?.fractionalPartWithNDigits(9)
 37â†’        }
 38â†’}
 39â†’
 40â†’private object TimeFields {
 41â†’    val hour = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hour), minValue = 0, maxValue = 23)
 42â†’    val minute = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::minute), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59)
 43â†’    val second =
 44â†’        UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::second), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59, defaultValue = 0)
 45â†’    val fractionOfSecond =
 46â†’        GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::fractionOfSecond), defaultValue = DecimalFraction(0, 9))
 47â†’    val amPm = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::amPm))
 48â†’    val hourOfAmPm = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hourOfAmPm), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
 49â†’}
 50â†’
 51â†’internal class IncompleteLocalTime(
 52â†’    override var hour: Int? = null,
 53â†’    override var hourOfAmPm: Int? = null,
 54â†’    override var amPm: AmPmMarker? = null,
 55â†’    override var minute: Int? = null,
 56â†’    override var second: Int? = null,
 57â†’    override var nanosecond: Int? = null
 58â†’) : TimeFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalTime> {
 59â†’    fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime {
 60â†’        val hour: Int = hour?.let { hour ->
 61â†’            hourOfAmPm?.let {
 62â†’                require((hour + 11) % 12 + 1 == it) { "Inconsistent hour and hour-of-am-pm: hour is $hour, but hour-of-am-pm is $it" }
 63â†’            }
 64â†’            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 65â†’                require((amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) == (hour >= 12)) {
 66â†’                    "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is $hour, but the AM/PM marker is $amPm"
 67â†’                }
 68â†’            }
 69â†’            hour
 70â†’        } ?: hourOfAmPm?.let { hourOfAmPm ->
 71â†’            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 72â†’                hourOfAmPm.let { if (it == 12) 0 else it } + if (amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) 12 else 0
 73â†’            }
 74â†’        } ?: throw DateTimeFormatException("Incomplete time: missing hour")
 75â†’        return LocalTime(
 76â†’            hour,
 77â†’            requireParsedField(minute, "minute"),
 78â†’            second ?: 0,
 79â†’            nanosecond ?: 0,
 80â†’        )
 81â†’    }
 82â†’
 83â†’    fun populateFrom(localTime: LocalTime) {
 84â†’        hour = localTime.hour
 85â†’        hourOfAmPm = (localTime.hour + 11) % 12 + 1
 86â†’        amPm = if (localTime.hour >= 12) AmPmMarker.PM else AmPmMarker.AM
 87â†’        minute = localTime.minute
 88â†’        second = localTime.second
 89â†’        nanosecond = localTime.nanosecond
 90â†’    }
 91â†’
 92â†’    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(hour, hourOfAmPm, amPm, minute, second, nanosecond)
 93â†’
 94â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 95â†’        other is IncompleteLocalTime && hour == other.hour && hourOfAmPm == other.hourOfAmPm && amPm == other.amPm &&
 96â†’            minute == other.minute && second == other.second && nanosecond == other.nanosecond
 97â†’
 98â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int =
 99â†’        (hour ?: 0) * 31 + (hourOfAmPm ?: 0) * 31 + (amPm?.hashCode() ?: 0) * 31 + (minute ?: 0) * 31 +
100â†’            (second ?: 0) * 31 + (nanosecond ?: 0)
101â†’
102â†’    override fun toString(): String =
103â†’        "${hour ?: "??"}:${minute ?: "??"}:${second ?: "??"}.${
104â†’            nanosecond?.let { nanos ->
105â†’                nanos.toString().let { it.padStart(9 - it.length, '0') }
106â†’            } ?: "???"
107â†’        }"
108â†’}
109â†’
110â†’internal interface AbstractWithTimeBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime {
111â†’    fun addFormatStructureForTime(structure: FormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>)
112â†’
113â†’    override fun hour(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(HourDirective(padding)))
114â†’    override fun amPmHour(padding: Padding) =
115â†’        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmHourDirective(padding)))
116â†’
117â†’    override fun amPmMarker(am: String, pm: String) =
118â†’        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmMarkerDirective(am, pm)))
119â†’
120â†’    override fun minute(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(MinuteDirective(padding)))
121â†’    override fun second(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(SecondDirective(padding)))
122â†’    override fun secondFraction(minLength: Int, maxLength: Int) =
123â†’        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(FractionalSecondDirective(minLength, maxLength)))
124â†’
125â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
126â†’    override fun time(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>) = when (format) {
127â†’        is LocalTimeFormat -> addFormatStructureForTime(format.actualFormat)
128â†’    }
129â†’}

131â†’private class HourDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
132â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
133â†’        TimeFields.hour,
134â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
135â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
136â†’    ) {
137â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
138â†’        get() = when (padding) {
139â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}()"
140â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
141â†’        }
142â†’
143â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is HourDirective && padding == other.padding
144â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
145â†’}
146â†’
147â†’private class AmPmHourDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
148â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
149â†’        TimeFields.hourOfAmPm, minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
150â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
151â†’    ) {
152â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
153â†’        get() = when (padding) {
154â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}()"
155â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
156â†’        }
157â†’
158â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is AmPmHourDirective && padding == other.padding
159â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()

162â†’private class AmPmMarkerDirective(private val amString: String, private val pmString: String) :
163â†’    NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer, AmPmMarker>(
164â†’        TimeFields.amPm, mapOf(

171â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
172â†’        get() =

174â†’

191â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::minute.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"

215â†’internal class FractionalSecondDirective(
216â†’    private val minDigits: Int,
217â†’    private val maxDigits: Int,
218â†’    zerosToAdd: List<Int> = NO_EXTRA_ZEROS,
219â†’) :
220â†’    DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
221â†’        TimeFields.fractionOfSecond,
222â†’        minDigits,
223â†’        maxDigits,
224â†’        zerosToAdd
225â†’    ) {
226â†’
227â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
228â†’        get() {
229â†’            val ref = "secondFraction" // can't directly reference `secondFraction` due to resolution ambiguity
230â†’            // we ignore `grouping`, as it's not representable in the end users' code
231â†’            return when {
232â†’                minDigits == 1 && maxDigits == 9 -> "$ref()"
233â†’                minDigits == 1 -> "$ref(maxLength = $maxDigits)"
234â†’                maxDigits == 1 -> "$ref(minLength = $minDigits)"
235â†’                maxDigits == minDigits -> "$ref($minDigits)"
236â†’                else -> "$ref($minDigits, $maxDigits)"
